articles.title: The $100,000 question: Navigating the new H-1B lottery system
articles.summary: The new H-1B lottery system replaces the traditional random selection with a wage-weighted approach, giving higher-paid positions a better chance of selection. This change could significantly impact employers and foreign professionals seeking H-1B visas.
keyPoints: New H-1B lottery system is wage-weighted.
Higher-paid positions have increased chances of selection.
Employers and foreign professionals need to adapt to these changes.

articles.title: Likely Revamp of F-1 Opt Puts Indian Students in Limbo
articles.summary: The US Department of Homeland Security is reviewing the F-1 OPT program, which allows international students to work in the US for up to 12 months after graduation. This review has raised concerns and uncertainty for many Indian students currently in the program.
keyPoints: DHS is actively reviewing the F-1 OPT program.
Concerns are rising among Indian students regarding their future.
The program currently allows work for up to 12 months post-graduation.

articles.title: F-1 Opt Speculated To Be Revamped, Thousands of Indian Students in Limbo
articles.summary: The ongoing review of the F-1 OPT program by the DHS is causing anxiety among Indian students who rely on this program for employment in the US after graduation. The potential changes could significantly impact their ability to work.
keyPoints: DHS is considering changes to the F-1 OPT program.
Indian students are particularly affected by this uncertainty.
The program is crucial for post-graduation employment.

articles.title: Judge rules lawsuit can proceed against Trump-era student visa revocations
articles.summary: A federal judge has allowed a lawsuit challenging the Trump administration's mass revocation of international student visas to proceed, which could have implications for many affected students.
keyPoints: Lawsuit against Trump-era visa revocations can proceed.
Federal judge's ruling may benefit affected international students.
Challenges policies allowing visa revocations based on criminal database hits.

articles.title: H-1B visa registration for fiscal year 2027 ends: What's next?
articles.summary: The registration period for H-1B visas for fiscal year 2027 has ended, and the USCIS will now conduct the selection process. Lottery notifications are expected by March 31, which will determine who gets the visas.
keyPoints: H-1B registration period has ended.
USCIS will conduct the selection process.
Lottery notifications expected by March 31.

articles.title: H-1B visa registration for fiscal year 2027 ends: What's next?
articles.summary: The registration period for H-1B visas for fiscal year 2027 has ended, and the USCIS will now conduct the selection process. Lottery notifications are expected by March 31, which is crucial for international students seeking employment.
keyPoints: H-1B registration period has ended.
USCIS will conduct the selection process.
Lottery notifications expected by March 31.

articles.title: Significant Immigration Policy Changes Announced for April 2026
articles.summary: The Trump administration has announced substantial changes to U.S. immigration policy set to take effect in April 2026, which may impact various visa categories, including those relevant to international students.
keyPoints: Substantial changes to U.S. immigration policy announced.
Changes set to take effect in April 2026.
May impact various visa categories, including student visas.

articles.title: Know the Law: The wage-powered H-1B lottery effective Feb. 27, 2026
articles.summary: The new wage-level H-1B lottery system changes how employers and foreign professionals approach the visa application process. This column discusses the implications of these changes and what they mean for applicants.
keyPoints: The H-1B lottery is now wage-level based.
This change affects how employers hire foreign professionals.
Understanding the new system is crucial for applicants.

articles.title: Trump is threatening international students, and a new bill could help stop him
articles.summary: A new bipartisan bill has been introduced to codify the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program, which allows international students to work in their field of study. This legislation aims to protect the rights of international students amid ongoing threats.
keyPoints: Bipartisan bill introduced to protect OPT.
Allows international students to work in their field for 12 months.
Extensions available for STEM students.

articles.title: US May Revamp OPT, Impacting Thousands Of Indian Students
articles.summary: The DHS is re-evaluating the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program, which could lead to significant changes affecting thousands of foreign graduates, particularly from India. This review has raised concerns about future employment opportunities.
keyPoints: DHS is re-evaluating the OPT program.
Potential changes could affect thousands of foreign graduates.
Indian students are among the most impacted.

articles.title: New Student Visas Dropped 35.6% Last Summer
articles.summary: Data from the State Department shows a significant drop in new student visas issued last summer, following a temporary freeze on visa interviews. This decline could have long-term implications for international student enrollment in US institutions.
keyPoints: New student visas dropped by 35.6%
Impact of previous visa interview freeze
Potential long-term effects on international student enrollment

articles.title: H-1B visa lottery with new $100,000 fees launches
articles.summary: The Trump administration's overhaul of the US visa system for highly skilled immigrants is being tested with the new H-1B lottery. This year's lottery introduces a $100,000 fee and changes to the selection process, impacting many international professionals seeking employment in the US.
keyPoints: New $100,000 fee for H-1B lottery applications
Changes to the selection process favor higher-paid positions
Impacts international professionals seeking work in the US

articles.title: US to re-evaluate optional practical training, here is how it will affect international students
articles.summary: The US Department of Homeland Security is reviewing the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program, which allows international students to work in the US after graduation. This review could lead to significant changes in the program, affecting many international students' post-study work opportunities.
keyPoints: DHS is conducting a review of the OPT program
Potential changes could affect international students' work rights
Review follows concerns from various stakeholders

articles.title: Preparing for the 2026 H-1B Cap Registration Season
articles.summary: As the H-1B cap filing season approaches, employers sponsoring foreign workers for H-1B visas are advised to prepare for the electronic registration process. This year's registration includes important procedural changes that employers need to be aware of.
keyPoints: H-1B cap filing season is approaching
Employers should prepare for electronic registration
Includes important procedural changes
